activities_projects.title: In-depth Character Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze Amanda’s personality and psyche deeply.
steps: Identify Amanda’s traits, emotions, and contradictions.
Cite lines from the poem that reveal her imagination, fear, and frustration.
Write a 1-paragraph critical analysis connecting her personality to the poet’s message.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will critically evaluate the character using textual evidence.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, textual references, and insightfulness.

activities_projects.title: Exploring Poetic Devices
activities_projects.objective: Examine the poet’s use of literary techniques.
steps: Identify devices like repetition, imagery, parenthesis, and tone.
Explain how each device reflects Amanda’s inner thoughts and emotions.
Write examples from the text with detailed explanations.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop analytical skills in literary techniques.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification, relevance, and depth of explanation.

activities_projects.title: Parent-Child Perspective Essay
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the poem from dual perspectives.
steps: Write an essay exploring the parent’s expectations versus Amanda’s imagination.
Explain how the parent’s tone influences Amanda’s feelings.
Include textual examples to support your argument.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop critical thinking by examining multiple perspectives.
activities_projects.evaluation: Coherence, use of textual evidence, and depth of analysis.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of Imagination
activities_projects.objective: Compare Amanda’s imagination with real-life constraints.
steps: List her imagined characters and scenarios.
Analyze how these reflect her desire for freedom and escape.
Write a 5–6 sentence commentary linking imagination to psychological growth.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect imagination with character development and thematic depth.
activities_projects.evaluation: Insightfulness and clarity in linking imagination to themes.

activities_projects.title: Theme-based Critical Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Examine underlying themes like freedom, restriction, and growing up.
steps: Identify at least three central themes of the poem.
Provide lines from the poem that highlight each theme.
Write a short critical paragraph analyzing how the themes are conveyed.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen literary interpretation and thematic understanding.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of thematic analysis, textual evidence, and clarity.

activities_projects.title: Creative Perspective Writing
steps: Imagine a day in Amanda’s life not mentioned in the poem.
Write a diary entry reflecting her thoughts, dreams, and frustrations.
Include advanced vocabulary and literary techniques like metaphor or imagery.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance creative writing and perspective-taking skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, depth of character voice, and literary quality.

activities_projects.title: Poetic Device Presentation
activities_projects.objective: Teach peers about literary devices in the poem.
steps: Prepare a presentation identifying all poetic devices used in 'Amanda'.
Explain how each device contributes to meaning and emotion.
Present findings to the class with examples.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop presentation skills and deepen literary analysis.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, clarity, and ability to explain devices.

activities_projects.title: Analytical Poster – Parent vs Child
activities_projects.objective: Visualize conflict and interaction between parent and child.
steps: Create a poster showing the parent’s instructions and Amanda’s responses.
Use quotes, symbols, and colors to depict emotions.
Write 2–3 lines summarizing the parent-child dynamic.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students synthesize textual understanding into visual representation.

activities_projects.title: Critical Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Discuss deeper meanings and societal implications.
steps: Discuss why children’s imagination is important in growing up.
Analyze societal or parental pressures reflected in the poem.
Share your opinions with textual support in class discussion.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will engage in critical thinking and discussion skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Insightfulness, textual support, and active participation.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Essay – Childhood in Literature
activities_projects.objective: Compare Amanda with another child character in literature.
steps: Choose a child character from any other poem or story you have read.
Compare their thoughts, imagination, and reactions to Amanda.
Write a structured essay highlighting similarities, differences, and lessons learned.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen comparative literary skills and critical reasoning.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison, textual references, and coherence.
